text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-datepicker repository (a React date-picker component library) at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: `DatePicker` accepts an explicit `timeZone` prop (an IANA zone name such as "Pacific/Kiritimati") meant to pin the picker to a timezone different from the browser's local timezone. When that `timeZone` differs significantly from the local/browser timezone, the picker gets the calendar day wrong in three places: (1) clicking a day in the calendar grid produces an `onChange` date, and a visually 'selected' day, that are off by one day (in the configured timeZone) from the day the user actually clicked; (2) the formatted value shown in the text input is off by one day because it reflects UTC/browser-local time instead of the configured timeZone; (3) with `selectsMultiple`, the calendar does not consistently highlight the correct set of days as selected in the target timezone. In short, calendar day-highlighting, the input's formatted display value, and multi-date-selection highlighting must all consistently interpret `selected` / `startDate` / `endDate` / `selectedDates` (and the values handed to `onChange`) in terms of the configured `timeZone`, not the browser's local timezone. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: this is a Node/TypeScript project managed with Yarn (Berry) via Corepack; dependencies are already installed (re-run `corepack enable && yarn install --immutable` if you ever need to). Tests use Jest — run a single file with `node_modules/.bin/jest <path>`.

text: I’ll trace the existing `timeZone` handling through date construction, formatting, and selection predicates, then patch the shared source paths and run focused Jest coverage. I’ll also check prior Nowledge context for any repository-specific timezone decisions before changing behavior.

If no format matches, non-strict parsing (strictParsing=false) falls back to native new Date(value), while strict parsing returns null without native fallback. This preserves format precedence and restores flexible typed/pasted input such as ISO dates with an MM/dd/yyyy format.","relevance_score":0.7054337919508427,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (58%) + Text Match (31%) + Keyword Match (12%)"},{"id":"5ea15197-175d-494b-ad85-21ec42235bb8","memory_id":"5ea15197-175d-494b-ad85-21ec42235bb8","title":"Downshift getItemProps aria-selected preserves boolean type","content":"In Downshift, useCombobox and useSelect getItemProps must return aria-selected as a real boolean. String interpolation (`${comparison}`) made both true and false values strings, violating typings and causing strict comparisons to fail. Returning the comparison directly fixes the JS props object while React DOM serialization remains aria-selected=\"true\"/\"false\".","relevance_score":0.5432776510480655,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (69%) + Text Match (24%) + Keyword Match (7%)"},{"id":"3a3a09a0-4b07-4d16-b761-94522728d385","memory_id":"3a3a09a0-4b07-4d16-b761-94522728d385","title":"useCombobox blur with null relatedTarget","content":"In Downshift useCombobox, input blur selection must distinguish browser focus loss from deliberate focus movement: dispatch InputBlur with selectItem false when the blur event's relatedTarget is explicitly null, allowing the reducer to close the menu and clear the highlight without changing selectedItem. Keep selectItem true for non-null relatedTarget so tabbing/clicking to another page element preserves selection behavior.","relevance_score":0.4700365351154146,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (70%) + Text Match (17%) + Keyword Match (14%)"},{"id":"205762a2-6728-48b1-bf7d-346406869adf","memory_id":"205762a2-6728-48b1-bf7d-346406869adf","title":"useSelect type-ahead spaces and inclusive multi-character matching","content":"In downshift useSelect type-ahead, Space must retain its normal toggle/select behavior only when no query is active; when inputValue is non-empty, the Space key should dispatch ToggleButtonKeyDownCharacter so spaces accumulate in multi-character labels. Character matching should skip the highlighted item only for a one-character query; for queries of length 2 or more, scan inclusively from the current highlight so the current item remains a valid match. text: The repository already has timezone conversion helpers and partial `DatePicker` integration. The likely gap is that child calendar components receive raw prop dates while their day-level comparisons and formatting still use browser-local getters; I’m reading those call paths now to identify the smallest consistent fix.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' src/calendar.tsx 2>/dev/null || true; rg -n \"<Calendar|selected=|startDate=|endDate=|selectedDates=|formatDate\\(\" src/index.tsx src/calendar_container.tsx src/month.tsx src/year.tsx"

text: The fix will preserve the public contract of `Date` values as instants while giving the calendar a timezone-local “wall-clock” view of `selected`, range endpoints, and multiple selections. I’ll also make the existing safe formatting helpers timezone-aware so single, range, and multiple input values use the same interpretation; this keeps custom multiple-date formatters aligned too.

text: The first verification exposed only a type boundary: this codebase intentionally stores a narrowed locale type, while `date-fns-tz` expects the full `date-fns` locale type. I’m correcting that cast at the adapter boundary, then I’ll run the focused suites again and add explicit LA/Kiritimati assertions for the three reported surfaces.

Both focused suites and the TypeScript check now pass. One remaining consistency point is multi-date toggling: its duplicate/removal check still compares raw dates with browser-local `isSameDay`, so I’m aligning that comparison with the configured zone as well; this matters when two UTC instants straddle a local/browser midnight but belong to the same target calendar day.

input.title: react-datepicker timeZone display and selection boundary
input.content: react-datepicker DatePicker must convert selected, startDate, endDate, and selectedDates to the configured IANA timeZone before passing them to Calendar, while calendar clicks are converted back with fromZonedTime for onChange. safeDateFormat, range, and multiple formatting must use formatInTimeZone, and selectsMultiple duplicate/removal comparisons must compare target-zone wall dates.

text:
Fixed the timezone handling in `src/`:

- Calendar selection/highlighting now converts `selected`, range dates, and multiple dates into the configured timezone.
- Input formatting supports timezone-aware single, range, and multiple values.
- Multiple-date toggling compares calendar days in the configured timezone.
- Existing click results continue converting back to UTC instants for callbacks.

Verification passed:

- `yarn tsc --noEmit`
- `jest` timezone and multiple-date suites: 49 tests passed
- `git diff --check`
